notes: Active-region size cap (px). 50000 is the lab default for cross-comparability. Lower (e.g. 2000) if hyperactive files hang. Higher (inf) only if you accept potential hang risk on dense recordings.
Minimum active-region size (px). Default 20 is appropriate for most calcium imaging.
Threshold for active-region scaling. Default 2 (AQuA2 standard).
Minimum event duration in frames. Default 3 (AQuA2 standard).
Detect global signal (1=on, 0=off). Turn off only if you hit 'Index exceeds array elements' on short/sparse recordings (Case 3 lesson).
Global signal window duration in frames. Default 20.
Seconds per frame. Set to 1/Hz for your acquisition. Examples: 20 Hz -> 0.05; 10 Hz -> 0.1; 1.55 Hz -> 0.645
Micrometers per pixel. Microscope/objective dependent. Examples: 20x typical -> 1.3; 10x typical -> 2.6
